## Summary

Incremys's March 2026 content calendar guide provides the most structured, European-market-calibrated treatment of content planning in the research set. Its value is in the explicit cadence recommendations by resource level and the concrete workflow template (brief → writing → editing → SEO → publishing). The guide distinguishes between the publishing plan ("go-live dates" view) and the production calendar ("make it happen" view with milestones and dependencies) - a distinction that helps founders understand why a simple spreadsheet often isn't enough.

The cadence recommendations by team size are the most conservative and honest in the research set - which makes them more credible for the stinger's solo founder audience: Solo (or one person + occasional expert): "2 to 4 posts per month, with one monthly pillar piece and shorter supporting content." Small team: "1 post per week, plus an update of an older piece every two weeks." Agency + internal approvals: "4 publications per month" as a stable pipeline. These are notably lower than the Averi guide's 1-2/week for solo founders, suggesting a range of 4-8 per month is realistic for a solo founder.

The milestone framework is practical: brief approval at D-14, first draft at D-7, SEO optimization + upload at D-3, publish + distribute on day of. This 2-week production runway is a concrete minimum standard for any planned content. The guide also introduces "buffer" content (2-4 items ready to publish, evergreen topics, short-form fallbacks) as a system for maintaining cadence when approvals or blockers interrupt the pipeline.

## Key data points / quotes

- Solo cadence: "2 to 4 posts per month, with one monthly pillar piece and shorter supporting content."
- Small team: "1 post per week, plus an update of an older piece every two weeks."
- "A simple rule: pick a rhythm you can sustain with editing and optimisation. A cadence you keep for six months beats a sprint followed by a long gap."
- Production timeline: D-14 (brief) → D-7 (first draft) → D-3 (SEO + upload) → D (publish + distribute).
- Required calendar fields (beyond just dates): topic + angle, target keyword, intent, format, RACI, estimated effort, approval rules, milestones.
- Buffer strategy: "prepare 2-4 'buffer' items ready to publish; choose evergreen topics with minimal reliance on current events."
- Efficiency techniques: batching (ideation/briefing on one day), series (3-5 episodes per theme), repurposing, planned updates.
- Refresh cadence: "monthly or quarterly" update slots for content that already performs.

## Implications for stinger-forge

- The solo founder cadence range of 2-4 posts per month (one pillar + shorter cluster content) is the most honest benchmark and should be the anchor recommendation in `guides/07-cadence-planning.md`.
- The "2-week minimum production runway" (brief at D-14) is a concrete planning standard that helps founders avoid reactive publishing.
- The buffer content strategy (2-4 evergreen items ready to publish) is a resilience mechanism that belongs in the cadence planning guide.
- The batching efficiency technique (separate ideation day from drafting day) should be included as a practical implementation tip.
- The "monthly or quarterly refresh" cadence for existing content is a cadence item often overlooked - include as a separate line in the cadence planning model.
